 :: The detail page should have the scrape button injected into it. The html page source is 'app/plugin/docs/real-estate_29263294_3369-findlay-creek-drive-ottawa-2501-leitrim.html'. The list page urls look like this: 'https://www.realtor.ca/map". The list page content sample is here: 'apps/plugin/docs/realtor.ca_map.html'. We need to make sure our scrape buttons show up in both pages. We also want scrape icon or scrape button for each listing on the map page."

update the plan, the saved searches we can use to identify segments of properties to import/scrape can be found on 'realtor.ca' page at 'https://www.realtor.ca/my-saved-searches'. We need to scrape those probably behind the scenes in the  browser if possible, and get the names, the id, and urls and the type. We will show the saved searches in the user interface in some kind of list with the site name or site icon logo in the list. We want this link so we can batch import the properties into the extension and saved in the backend for the user. Here is a example of the saved searches page : 'apps/plugin/docs/realtor.ca_my-saved-searches.html'.
